I disagree because paradoxical excitement is classically associated with nitrous oxide (Option C), especially in children under age 5 due to its known central nervous system effects, including excitation followed by depression. Nitrous oxide can induce transient CNS stimulation leading to agitation or excitability—a well-documented phenomenon distinct from any respiratory disease process.

Your chosen answer: **C**
